pages.text: Rotation has a $(bold)speed$(), measured in RPM. Some machines simply need a minimum to run; others go faster the quicker they spin. A Mechanical Drill or Saw on a slow line barely works; spin it up and it flies.$(br2)Speed is also half of $(l:1_power/stress)stress$() — the faster a thing spins, the more force it draws.
pages.title: Speed

pages.text: $(bold)Changing speed:$()$(br)$(li)$(bold)Cogwheel ratios$() — large-to-small speeds up, small-to-large slows down.$(li)$(bold)Rotation Speed Controller$() — set an exact RPM on a large cog, dialled by redstone.$(li)$(bold)Gearshift$() — reverses direction on a redstone signal.$(li)$(bold)Clutch$() — cuts power on a signal without stopping the source.
pages.title: Controlling Speed
